The Hippocrene Spring

On Mount Helicon, the winged horse Pegasus struck the ground with his hoof, and from that spot the Hippocrene spring began to flow. The Muses were said to gather by its waters.

Poets later treated the spring as a source of inspiration, so that to drink from the Hippocrene meant to receive the gift of verse. The image recurs throughout classical and renaissance literature.

This second paragraph exists so the document has enough real content for the Readability extractor to treat it as an article rather than chrome.
